title: Pseudolmedia ferruginea
intro: Pseudolmedia ferruginea, commonly known as the Ironwood or Iron Tree, is a species of plant in the family Moraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudolmedia ferruginea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudolmedia ferruginea is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and other topical creams.
taxonomy: Pseudolmedia ferruginea is classified within the family Moraceae, and its genus is part of the subfamily Moreae. It is a species of flowering plant, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the plant kingdom, Pseudolmedia ferruginea belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and its classification continues with the division Magnoliophyta. This plant is also known to be part of the class Magnoliopsida, and its order is Rosales. Pseudolmedia ferruginea's family is further classified under the clade angiosperms.
